segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Welcome to our Sprint 22 retro. Let's use the Start Stop Continue format. I'll give you a couple minutes for each section.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Let's start with START — what should we start doing?

segments.speaker: Nick
segments.text: We should start doing code reviews within 24 hours. Some of my PRs sat for 3 days last sprint and it blocked me.

segments.speaker: Olivia
segments.text: I agree. We should also start writing acceptance criteria before we start coding. I had to rework the search feature twice because the requirements changed.

segments.speaker: Paul
segments.text: We should start doing brief design docs for anything over 3 story points. The payment refactor was way more complex than we expected because we skipped the design phase.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Good suggestions. Let's move to STOP — what should we stop doing?

segments.speaker: Paul
segments.text: We need to stop merging PRs on Fridays. The payment bug from two weeks ago was merged Friday afternoon and we spent all of Monday firefighting.

segments.speaker: Nick
segments.text: Stop having meetings during focus time blocks. I had three interruptions during my deep work block on Wednesday.

segments.speaker: Olivia
segments.text: We should stop scope-creeping sprint items mid-sprint. The dashboard ticket went from 3 points to effectively 8 because we kept adding features.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Fair points all around. Now CONTINUE — what's working well?

segments.speaker: Olivia
segments.text: Continue doing pair programming for complex features. The session Nick and I had on the caching layer was really productive.

segments.speaker: Paul
segments.text: Continue the daily standups at 9:30. The new time slot works much better for everyone.

segments.speaker: Nick
segments.text: Continue using the shared testing environment. It caught two integration issues before they hit staging.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Great feedback. Let's pick 2-3 concrete improvements to commit to for next sprint.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: I'm hearing three strong ones: 24-hour PR review SLA, no Friday merges, and design docs for items over 3 points. Can we commit to all three?

segments.speaker: Nick
segments.text: The PR review SLA is my top pick. That alone would improve our flow significantly.

segments.speaker: Paul
segments.text: I'm in on all three. The no-Friday-merge rule is easy to enforce.

segments.speaker: Olivia
segments.text: Agreed. For the design docs, can we create a lightweight template so it doesn't become a burden?

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Good idea. I'll create a template. So our three commitments: 24-hour PR review SLA, no merges on Fridays, and design docs for 3+ point items with a lightweight template.

segments.speaker: Maria
segments.text: Thanks everyone. I'll add these as team agreements in our wiki. See you at sprint planning tomorrow!
